content.problemStatement:
The Petrozavodsk camp takes place in about one month. Jafar wants to participate in the camp, but guess what? His coach is Yalalovichik.

Yalalovichik is a legendary coach, famous in the history of competitive programming. However, he is only willing to send to the camp students who solve really hard problems on Timus. He started a marathon at the beginning of December. Initially, he said that people who solve 200 or more problems by the 31-st of December may go to the camp. Jafar made a schedule for the next month. For each day, he knows how many problems he is going to solve.

The problem is that Yalalovichik is a really moody coach ‚Äî he may wake up tomorrow and change his decision about the deadline and the number of problems that must be solved by this deadline to qualify for the camp. Jafar has QQQ such scenarios. Now he wants to know: in each scenario, if he does not change his problem solving schedule, will he go to the camp or not?
constraints: d1,d2,‚Ä¶,dDd_1, d_2, \ldots, d_Dd1‚Äã,d2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,dD‚Äã are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer DDD - the number of days in Jafar's schedule.
DDD lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§D1 \le i \le D1‚â§i‚â§D), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers did_idi‚Äã and pip_ipi‚Äã denoting that Jafar will solve pip_ipi‚Äã problems on day did_idi‚Äã.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ denoting the number of scenarios Jafar considers.
QQQ lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§Q1 \le i \le Q1‚â§i‚â§Q), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers deadidead_ideadi‚Äã and reqireq_ireqi‚Äã denoting a scenario where Yalaovichik decides that students who solve reqireq_ireqi‚Äã problems by day deadidead_ideadi‚Äã (inclusive) will go to the camp.
content.outputFormat: For each scenario, print a single line containing the string `"Go Camp"` if Jafar is going to the camp or `"Go Sleep"` otherwise (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

By the end of day 999, Jafar will not have any problem solved.
By the end of day 151515, Jafar will have 999 problems solved, which is enough to go to the camp, since he needs at least 777 problems.
